name: Container, AuthorizationServer, and custom JWT
outcome: A container wires Drizzle, the five repos, the custom JWT service, and a configured AuthorizationServer with authorization_code + refresh_token enabled.
scope: src/container.ts and the custom JWT service; no HTTP routes yet.
acceptance_criteria: container builds new AuthorizationServer(clientRepo, tokenRepo, scopeRepo, jwt, { requiresPKCE: true, requiresS256: true })
enableGrantTypes registers refresh_token and authorization_code (with authCodeRepository + userRepository) at 1h intervals, matching current behavior
MyCustomJwtService.extraTokenFields adds email and client name
steps: src/app/oauth/services/custom_jwt_service.ts — port MyCustomJwtService extending JwtService with extraTokenFields returning { email, client }; drop the Nest @Injectable and register factory

.env + .env.example — add JWT_SECRET (replacing the hardcoded 'super-secret' from oauth.module.ts:24); the new code reads it from process.env. NOTE .env and .env.example already exist with DATABASE_URL — extend both with JWT_SECRET rather than recreating them
src/container.ts — construct the postgres.js-backed Drizzle db, the five Drizzle repos, and MyCustomJwtService from process.env.JWT_SECRET

src/container.ts — build new AuthorizationServer(clientRepo, tokenRepo, scopeRepo, jwt, { requiresPKCE: true, requiresS256: true }) and call enableGrantTypes(['refresh_token', 1h], [{ grant: 'authorization_code', authCodeRepository, userRepository }, 1h])
src/container.ts — export the built server, db, and repos for route handlers and tests
notes: Replaces authorization_server.service.ts (no more extends/Nest factory). Plain AuthorizationServer, not a subclass. Old service files removed in cleanup.
